paragraphs.body: There exists a place, unknown yet intriguing, which fills me with uncertainty. What does the unknown of the ocean and my life have to do with each other? To others, it may not seem like much, but to me, the ocean is the perfect representation of the uncertain yet discoverable with time.

Last week I read an article by Claire Strickland titled “The benefits and risks of deep-sea exploration”. In the article, she talks about many different topics, but one fact in particular had an impact on me. Strickland writes that “only five percent of the sea is mapped, and right now, it is next to nearly impossible to explore the other ninety-five percent.

annotations.comment: This summary still feels incomplete. Don't forget the templates/writing moves we practiced in class!
annotations.context_left: exploration”. In the article, she talks about many different topics, but one fact in particular had

paragraphs.body: Throughout my life, I have been very comfortable and open with my family and friends because I trust them with my life. My immediate family members are my best friends, and I cannot imagine life without them. Ever since I was a child, my parents have told me that I could tell them anything no matter what the situation. My mom is genuinely my best friend, and as I grew older, I became less embarrassed to tell my friends that. My mother will always be the biggest role model in my life. The first day of high school was one of the hardest days of my life. I had gone to a public middle school, which did not send many kids to the high school I was attending. Suddenly, the friends I had grown up with would not be with me during the school day anymore, and the adjustment was extremely challenging. After the first day, I came home frustrated because making new friends was harder than I anticipated. However, when my mom saw me, she immediately asked what was wrong and comforted me. Although I thought I was upset because I did not have any friends, yet in hindsight, I now know that I was troubled by the unknown. I did not know whether I would successfully make friends, I did not know how I would perform in a new academic environment, and I did not know if I would even be happy in this new place.

annotations.text: did not know whether I would successfully make friends, I did not know how I would perform in a new academic environment, and I did not know if I would even be happy in this new place.

annotations.context_right: e. The first day of high sch

paragraphs.body: I was always taught to be trusting and supportive of others. After the way I felt after the first day, I could not comprehend how others were not raised the same way I had been. However, as time progressed, I became familiar with the unknowns of high school, and began to love Scranton Prep.

paragraphs.body: As I previously stated, the unknown could be intimidating for people. It begs the question, why can we not figure it out? I am currently in the midst of the unknown. I do not like this feeling, I do not like it here, and my mom is not here to comfort me. I am an adult? I have to live alone? It is all up to me to write my own story at this place called the University of Pittsburgh. Nobody is here telling me what to do, I am responsible for my own choices, whether they are good, bad, or indifferent. If I make an incorrect choice, I must live with the consequences. For some reason, I cannot wrap my head around it.

annotations.text: I am an adult? I have to live alone?
annotations.context_right: It is all up to me

paragraphs.body: I want to be a dentist, yet it is impossible to tell if that dream will become a reality. This part of the unknown frightens me and makes me feel uncomfortable. However, it is a part of life.

Once again, I am back to square one: at a new school, with few friends, and am yet to have trust in others. These thoughts scare me because I am in an unfamiliar place that is not twenty minutes away, but four and a half hours away from home.

paragraphs.body: Since starting college, I realized that growing up is indeed scary. Life throws you into situations that make you uncomfortable. You will end up making decisions you will later regret, but also decisions that you wish you made sooner. As I began my journey here at Pitt, I longed for a sense of home. I made endless calls to the people who brought me the most comfort, and I questioned if I was on the right path. Although it all felt uncomfortable, I knew it was a necessary part of life, and nothing, and no one can change that. Yes, it is true that the future will always be unknown, yet it is possible to take steps in the present to influence it. That is what makes life worth living, always progressing towards the future, and one day being able to look back on it all and be happy with yourself.

Once I felt more comfortable with my surroundings, I started to venture out more; simply meaning, taking the bus to get food, shop, and workout. Even with meeting new friends, I wanted to stay with what I knew, but venturing out meant meeting new people. I now realize that that was what I needed. I was not normally shy, but when it came to raising my hand in class, it was not going to be me; I was never going to be the girl answering questions because it made me anxious, and I felt embarrassed to mess up in front of strangers. Understanding how much I have grown and changed in this past month shows how much I have ventured into the unknown. It is definitely scary, but it is what you make of it. I would not change my new life here for anything. I love all my new friends and all the experiences I have had here make me grateful for embracing change.

annotations.text: simply meaning, taking the bus to get food, shop, and workout.
Understanding how much I have grown and changed in this past month shows how much I have ventured into the unknown.

paragraphs.body: In her Article on the ocean and the unknown Strickland claims, “This motivation, propelled by curiosity and the quest for knowledge, is why we venture beyond the familiar spaces of our everyday lives”. In other words, Strickland believes our intriguing thoughts are what make the higher ups do these impossible tasks. This quote explains the way I feel about starting my new chapter into the unknown. When scientists and biologists explore the ocean, they never know what they will find or what new creatures they will see. In everyday life, the new creatures they find could be compared to new friends or meeting new people. You never know what kind of person they will be. I get nervous to make new friends because I find it hard to build trust from the ground up, and sometimes branching out is challenging. In her Article on the ocean and the unknown, Strickland claims, “So much remains to be explored because reaching the bottom of the ocean is an incredibly difficult task”. Strickland is saying that at the end of the day, it is nearly impossible to explore everything. Likewise with my life, so much remains to be explored. This idea both excites and terrifies me. The possibilities of the future are exciting, yet the unknown is terrifying.

annotations.text: intriguing thoughts are what make the higher ups do these impossible tasks.
annotations.comment: This interpretation doesn't quite make sense? I'm not sure it accurately represents the quote.

paragraphs.body: There is so much in my life to still be explored. What does my future hold? What is out there that I am unaware of? Although it may seem frightening, I am honestly ecstatic to pursue my dreams of becoming a dentist, even if my aspirations change another five times! I have finally figured out what it all means: life is about exploring the unknown. Without the unknown, life would be pretty dull. That is why, I, finally, have made peace with it.

annotations.text: Without the unknown, life would be pretty dull.
